rulings.Case Title: THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC. VS. RENE PEINADO et al
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 21, 2025, line 1. PLAINTIFF THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC.'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. The Claim of Exemption by Amy McDougall, spouse of Judgment Debtor Rene Peinado, is GRANTED. McDougall made a prima facie showing that the funds at issue are exempt as spousal separate property and Judgment Creditor The National Collection Agency, Inc. did not submit evidence demonstrating or otherwise show the funds at issue are community property or otherwise not exempt. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 703.020, 703.080; see also Family Code sections 770.911(a), 1500, 1612.) For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: JULIANA LOURENCO LOPES DEMASI VS. GOLDEN GATE OBSTETRICS AND GYNECOLOGY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Default Judgment Against The Seven Defendants
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 21, 2025, line 8. PLAINTIFF JULIANA LOPES DEMASI'S Motion For Default Judgment Against The Seven Defendants. Plaintiff Juliana Lourenco Demasi's Motion for Default Judgment is DENIED. The court construes the motion as a motion for entry of default. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 585-587.) Default judgment requires that a party first obtain default(s), which is usually executed by the clerk, then pursue a default judgment in Department 505. The court is without jurisdiction over some or all of the claims because Plaintiff has filed notices of appeal and the appellate court has not yet issued a remittitur. The court notes it is without jurisdiction over the Defendant Regents and Defendant Golden Gate Obstetrics & Gynecology, Inc. The court is definitely without jurisdiction at least as to the Sutter Defendants, given Plaintiff's notice s of appeal. The motion is not properly noticed at all, given Plaintiff failed to file a Motion and Notice of Motion. The motion is not properly noticed under Code of Civil Procedure section 1005 and the rules for adding time based on the manner of service of the moving papers. Plaintiff failed timely to file proof of service of her papers. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1300.) Plaintiff has not carried her burden to secure a default against any Defendant. (See Code of Civil Procedure sections 585-587.) Any request to for an order finding Plaintiff vexatious must be presented in a properly noticed motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: ZAHIR NASERI VS. UBER TECHNOLOGIES, INC.
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 21, 2025, line 5. PLAINTIFF ZAHIR NASERI'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses. Plaintiff Zahir Naseri's Motion to Compel Discovery Responses, filed March 26, 2025, is DENIED. By his motion, Plaintiff seeks an order compelling Defendant Uber Technologies, Inc. to respond to his "Declaration of Person Most Qualified," served February 13, 2025. Plaintiff moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.290. "If a party to whom interrogatories are directed fails to serve a timely response, the following rules apply:  (b) The party propounding the interrogatories may move for an order compelling response to the interrogatories." Plaintiff moves, as well, under Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.310: Subdivision (a) of section 2031.310 provides: "On receipt of a response to a demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling, the demanding party may move for an order compelling further response to the demand if the demanding party deems that any of the following apply: (1) A statement of compliance with the demand is incomplete. (2) A representation of inability to comply is inadequate, incomplete, or evasive. (3) An objection in the response is without merit or too general." The statutes upon which Plaintiff basis his motion do not support the relief requested. Plaintiff's February 13, 2025, Declaration is not a set of interrogatories by any reasonable construction. Nor does it address a response to a request for production of documents. The court cannot reasonably construe it as a discovery demand that clearly triggers a required response. Nor on this record can the court grant Plaintiff relief under the California Discovery Act. The court does not find Plaintiff Zahir Naseri acted with substantial justification in connection with this purported discovery. Plaintiff Zahir Naseri shall pay $250 as sanctions, payable to the Clerk of the Court, payment no later than by June 13, 2025, receipt filed no later than June 20, 2025. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
